nodes.en: Blueprints and schematics are technical drawings showing construction, electrical, mechanical, or architectural design details. They translate design concepts into precise specifications for fabrication and assembly.
nodes.cs: Nákresy a schémata jsou technické výkresy ukazující stavební, elektrické, mechanické nebo architektonické detaily návrhu. Přeměňují koncepce návrhu na přesné specifikace pro výrobu a montáž.
nodes.brief:
Blueprints and schematics are technical drawings that specify how something is built: architectural plans, electrical wiring diagrams, mechanical assembly drawings, and structural details rendered in a shared graphical vocabulary of symbols, scales, and projections. They translate a design concept into the precise geometry and connectivity a fabricator or builder needs to reproduce it exactly.

Where [[09.03]] Technical Manuals explain how to operate or maintain a finished system, this node documents how the system was built in the first place — the drawing precedes the object. [[09.05]] Standards & Specifications define the dimensional tolerances, symbol conventions, and material requirements a drawing must conform to, and [[09.09]] Patents frequently embed schematic drawings as part of a legal disclosure, so the same drawing convention appears in both an engineering and an intellectual-property context. The discipline that produces these drawings sits within [[03]] Applied Sciences & Technology.

Reading and producing blueprints reliably depends on standardized drafting conventions. The [ISO technical drawing standards](https://www.iso.org/committee/49340.html) (ISO 128 and related) govern line types, projections, and symbols internationally, and the [American National Standards Institute](https://www.ansi.org/) publishes the parallel drafting standards used across US industry.
